sanskrit:
किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः।
तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात्॥
transliteration:
kiṃ karma kim akarmeti kavayo ’py atra mohitāḥ /
tat te karma pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvā mokṣyase ’śubhāt //
literal_meaning: What is action? What is inaction? Even the wise are confused about this. I shall explain to you that action, knowing which you will be freed from misfortune.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s mind is caught in a meta-cognitive loop: he can no longer trust his own definitions of ‘doing’ and ‘not-doing.’ Krishna signals that clarity comes only by stepping outside the ego’s frame of reference and aligning with a validated lineage of meaning.
psychological_state: Paralysis-by-analysis: the more Arjuna intellectualizes, the less he knows which lever to pull. His ego-narrative is temporarily decentered, opening space for authoritative re-education.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional is offered a promotion that requires ethically gray decisions; every option looks equally ‘active’ or ‘passive,’ and self-help books only deepen the fog.
anchor_text: True action is egoless alignment with dharma; all else is reactive motion.
